title: CRDs, Controllers, Operators, Admission Policies, Webhooks, and Extension Trust · Reconciliation Brief: K8s Extension Upgrade Trust
summary: A cluster extension stores an underspecified custom resource, mutates workloads through an unavailable webhook, and upgrades without conversion or rollback evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s extension upgrade trust increment in trusted extension API and controller contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate extension provenance, permissions, webhooks, CRDs, conversion, compatibility, backup, canary, observability, failure containment, and uninstall safety.
Distinguish aggregated APIs, CRDs, admission, scheduler plugins, device resource APIs, CNI, CSI, CRI, and Gateway implementations by authority and conformance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Extension Upgrade Trust · Theory K8s Extension Upgrade Trust
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall. Competency probe: Evaluate extension provenance, permissions, webhooks, CRDs, conversion, compatibility, backup, canary, observability, failure containment, and uninstall safety.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Extension Upgrade Trust contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict K8s Extension Upgrade Trust · Theory K8s Extension Upgrade Trust.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for evaluate extension provenance, permissions, webhooks, crds, conversion, compatibility, backup, canary, observability, failure containment, and uninstall safety. For Predict K8s Extension Upgrade Trust · Theory K8s Extension Upgrade Trust, keep K8s Extension Upgrade Trust connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

At Predict K8s Extension Upgrade Trust · Theory K8s Extension Upgrade Trust, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A theory CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall. options.text: Evaluate extension provenance, permissions, webhooks, CRDs, conversion, compatibility, backup, canary, observability, failure containment, and uninstall safety. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Deleting an operator safely removes all custom resources, external state, and admission dependencies. That claim does not survive the predict k8s extension upgrade trust evidence boundary.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall. Competency probe: Evaluate extension provenance, permissions, webhooks, CRDs, conversion, compatibility, backup, canary, observability, failure containment, and uninstall safety. A defensible solution applies K8s Extension Upgrade Trust, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to trusted extension API and controller contract.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-extensions-admission-operators-4
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-extensions-admission-operators-4
    artifact: extension-contract.yaml
    decision: worked k8s extensions admission operators 4
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      crd: structural-schema-with-status
      storage-version: v1
      conversion: tested
      controller: idempotent-rate-limited-reconcile
      finalizer: external-state-owned
      admission: validating-policy-first
      webhook: bounded-fail-policy
      uninstall: dependency-ordered
    verify: kubectl get crd,validatingadmissionpolicy,validatingwebhookconfiguration -o json
    failure: Reject schema-free CRDs, event-script controllers, unbounded webhooks, fail-open-by-default, or operator deletion as external-state cleanup.
    repair: Version and validate the API, test conversion, bound reconciliation and admission, preserve status, contain failure, and rehearse upgrade and uninstall.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Deleting an operator safely removes all custom resources, external state, and admission dependencies. This fails the K8s Extension Upgrade Trust contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate extension provenance, permissions, webhooks, CRDs, conversion, compatibility, backup, canary, observability, failure containment, and uninstall safety.
